notes: Bookkeeping refresh, not a quality retune: production runs SONNET_MODEL='claude-sonnet-5' (since chore/upgrade-sonnet-5-model), but this file still recorded 'claude-sonnet-4-6' on an older prompt_version, so the eval-smoke gate (PX-13) and full-suite regression report were comparing Sonnet-5 output against a stale Sonnet-4.6/2026-05-24.4 reference (phantom drift). A 2026-07-05 single-variable A/B (same prompt, 4.6 vs 5) already showed no material regression; this capture just re-anchors the reference to the current model+prompt. Methodology: ONE run of `python evals/runner.py --suite synthetic` (generate mode only; schema_version 3 supports mean/stdev/n/min/max per cell but does not carry a mode dimension, and assemble-mode scores are a structurally different population per runner.py's F-11 docstring that the comparison logic doesn't support splitting -- so this baseline covers generate-mode only, matching the file's pre-existing scope). n=1 per (fixture x rubric) therefore reads as stdev=0.0/min=max=mean, not five-run stability; treat this as a re-anchored reference point, not a revalidated confidence interval. fixture_set_hash unchanged (fixtures untouched) confirms this is a like-for-like model/prompt swap, not a fixture change. pm-senior's first attempt (20260709_173755Z.jsonl) hit a Haiku judge_error (invalid JSON) on the new callback_likelihood rubric; that fixture was re-run standalone (20260709_174652Z.jsonl, run_id 24b12daa4840) and this baseline uses the clean second attempt for all six pm-senior rubrics so the fixture's cells come from one internally-consistent run. callback_likelihood (commit 8640773) had no prior baseline entry; seeded fresh here at 4.2-4.6 across fixtures. Known below-4.0 cell carried forward and NOT indicative of the model swap: sre-mid-level x clarification_quality (3.2 -- Haiku judge noise on the UNCHANGED clarify path; single-run judge variance of this magnitude is expected per the v1.0.1 baseline's own run-to-run spread). sre-mid-level x iteration_quality (3.8, n=1) is below 4.0 but close to the prior baseline's 3.73 n=3 -- known fixture fragility, not new. ats_format/grounding/keyword_coverage/tone all >= 4.2 across all three fixtures. eval-smoke self-comparison (--subset smoke, grounding only) confirmed clean against this file.
